cards.front: A [...] is a network link where data transmission occurs in only one direction.
Unidirectional links are typically caused by Layer [...] issues.
[STP optional feature] protects against loops caused by unidirectional links.
When a Loop Guard-enabled port's Max Age counts down to 0, it enters the [...] state.

Enable Loop Guard on a port:
SW1(config-if)# [...]

Disable Loop Guard on a port:
SW1(config-if)# [...]
On which ports does the command spanning-tree loopguard default enabled Loop Guard?
Loop Guard and Root Guard [can/can't] be enabled on the same port at the same time.
If you configure spanning-tree guard loop on a port and then spanning-tree guard root, which feature is active on the port?
cards.back:
Root Guard
*the second command replaces the first

cards.front: If you configure spanning-tree loopguard default in global config mode and then spanning-tree guard root on a port, which feature is active on the port?
When will a Loop Guard-enabled port automatically recover?
cards.back: When it starts receiving BPDUs again
